title: Intensely Garlicky Norwegian Celeriac Gratin
description: A rich and creamy Norwegian dish featuring celeriac, garlic, and a mother-sauce base, perfect for a quick and satisfying vegetarian meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the sliced celeriac to the saucepan and cook for 5 minutes, until slightly tender.
In a separate saucepan, whisk together the flour and milk, and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens.
Remove the sauce from the heat and stir in the grated cheese, salt, and pepper.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of celeriac slices.
Pour the cheese sauce over the celeriac, then repeat the layers two more times, finishing with a layer of cheese on top.
Dot the top of the gratin with the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 30-40 minutes, until golden brown and bubbly.
Remove the gratin from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
